findings.fix: Split into an `AffineGroupSchemeOfFiniteType` (finite type only) and a separate smoothness predicate/hypothesis, or add the smoothness as a non-extending named assumption; record the standalone `IsGeometricallyReduced` so non-smooth groups like μ_p can be carried.
findings.issue: `AffineAlgGroup` bundles `Algebra.FiniteType` with `Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced`, i.e. it bakes smoothness into the class. The roadmap's Standing hypotheses explicitly say "do not bundle them into one mega-class", "Keep two distinct notions, and never make smoothness implicit", and the Design notes say "Don't bundle typeclasses; admit non-smooth/non-reduced groups (μ_p) and make smoothness an explicit hypothesis where used." This class excludes the very non-smooth/non-reduced examples (μ_p, α_p) the roadmap wants the theory to admit.

findings.evidence: roadmap/TauCetiRoadmap/ReductiveGroups/README.md lines 181-188 ("correct, geometric definition: g ∈ G(k̄) is unipotent iff ρ_g − id is nilpotent for every f.d. representation … A naïve `IsUnipotent` that tests nilpotence in the reduced ring A is vacuous").
findings.fix: Either defer `IsUnipotent` to the follow-up that builds the comodule / representation machinery (and remove it from this PR), or state the predicate as a placeholder that the docstring marks as the wrong definition so it is not depended on.
findings.issue: `IsUnipotent` is the "naïve `IsUnipotent` that tests nilpotence in the reduced ring A" that the roadmap itself flags with ⚠ as *vacuous* (only `g = 1` qualifies), with the correct definition stated to require comodule theory (Layer 1). Shipping this name with the wrong definition creates a stable API the rest of the tower will have to override; it does not advance Layer 5.

findings.evidence: roadmap/TauCetiRoadmap/ReductiveGroups/README.md lines 130-134 (Layer 0 R-points) and 243 ("Functor of points is the notion of points, not GroupLike, not just k-points").
findings.fix: Define `AlgPoints R A := A →ₐ[k] R` (matching Layer 0's prescription) and either omit `k`-points or define it as the specialization `AlgPoints k A`. Drop the `GroupLike k A` argument to `IsUnipotent`; the unipotent predicate should take a `k̄`-point.
findings.issue: `AlgPoints k A := A →ₐ[k] k` is the `k`-points-only specialization. The roadmap's Design notes say "Functor of points is the notion of points, not GroupLike, not just k-points", and Layer 0 calls out generalizing to `A →ₐ[k] R` for every commutative `k`-algebra `R` as the right default. Establishing the `k`-points name as the points type now will make the eventual functor-of-points refactor a breaking change.

findings.evidence: Reductive.lean lines 59-78: `unipotent_radical_trivial : True`, `radical_trivial : True`, the theorem proving `True` from `h.unipotent_radical_trivial`, and the instance `:= SemisimpleGroup.toReductiveGroup`.
findings.fix: Cut the `True`-field stubs from this PR; they belong in the Layer 6 PR that actually constructs the unipotent/radical and the `R_u(G_{k̄}) = 1` condition, or leave a `TODO` with the type signature only.
findings.issue: `ReductiveGroup` and `SemisimpleGroup` carry only `True` fields, so they are not class constraints at all — every `AffineAlgGroup` would satisfy them vacuously. The `ReductiveGroup.unipotentRadical_trivial'` and `SemisimpleGroup.instReductiveGroup` "results" prove `True`. This adds API surface without advancing the Layer 6 targets (radical `R(G)`, centre, derived group, central isogenies).

summary: PR is in scope for the ReductiveGroups roadmap (Layer 5/6), but the `AffineAlgGroup` class bundles smoothness into the typeclass, `IsUnipotent` is the naïve vacuous definition the roadmap itself flags with ⚠, and `AlgPoints` is `k`-points-only — each of these contradicts the roadmap's own design notes, so as written the PR does not advance the targets it claims to.
